author: thomkav
text: cusip._alphabet is too restrictive: prevents matching valid CUSIPs

author: thomkav
text:
The `cusip` module's `is_valid` function returns false for a CUSIP that I would expect would pass, as I found it on a real Fidelity statement (see screenshot of document sample).

```
cusip.is_valid(`FDIC99425`)
>> False
```

I believe this is because `cusip._alphabet` does not permit "I" or "O":

```
cusip._alphabet
>> '0123456789ABCDEFGH JKLMN PQRSTUVWXYZ*@#'
```

[![Screen Shot 2022-10-16 at 11 50 45 AM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/29004842/196052775-cd61943e-43f9-437e-aabb-8038f13bf472.png)](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/29004842/196052775-cd61943e-43f9-437e-aabb-8038f13bf472.png)

I checked [cusip.com](https://www.cusip.com/identifiers.html#/CUSIP) and I didn't find anything indicating that those two characters were not allowed.

author: arthurdejong
text: Thanks for pointing this out. It's been fixed in [1364e19](https://github.com/arthurdejong/python-stdnum/commit/1364e1917750b1515cb1b1662c24bc1080c4ecbf)
